NCT03021499.name: AURORA 1
NCT03021499.group: Active lupus nephritis class III/IV/V, voclosporin 23.7 mg PO BID + MMF + steroid taper vs placebo + MMF + steroid taper (complete renal response at 52 wk, OR)

allOutcomes.title: Complete renal response at week 52 (primary, composite UPCR <=0.5 mg/mg + eGFR >=60 or no decrease >20% + low-dose steroid + no rescue therapy)

NCT03021499.snippet: Source: Rovin BH et al. Lancet 2021;397:2070-2080 (AURORA 1).

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; max age 75 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Key Inclusion Criteria:~- Subjects with evidence of active nephritis, defined as follows:~* Kidney biopsy result within 2 years prior to screening indicating Class III, IV-S, IV-G (alone or in combination with Class V), or Class V LN with a doubling or greater increase of UPCR within the last 6 months to a minimum of ≥1.5 mg/mg for Class III/IV or to a minimum of ≥2 mg/mg for Class V at screening. Biopsy results over 6 months prior to screening must be reviewed with a medical monitor to confir...

title: RapidMeta Rheum/Renal | Voclosporin (Calcineurin Inhibitor) in Lupus Nephritis v1.0
